dtcs.description: Front Sensor Communication Malfunction This DTC is stored when there is an open or short circuit in the communication line between the front sensors and the ECU, or when there is a malfunction in a front sensor. HINT *1: w/o Advanced parking guidance system *2: w/ Advanced parking guidance system PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: SYSTEM CHECK (a) Check if the vehicle is equipped with the advanced parking guidance system. Result B --  CHECK DTC OUTPUT A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Intuitive Parking Assist System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Intuitive Parking Assist System. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CLEARANCE WARNING ECU (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE CLEARANCE WARNING ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Clearance Warning ECU (w/o Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CLEARANCE WARNING ECU - FRONT LEFT SIDE SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the L19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the D1 sensor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FRONT LEFT SIDE SENSOR - FRONT LEFT CORNER SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the D1 and D2 sensor connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FRONT LEFT CORNER SENSOR - FRONT LEFT CENTER SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the D2 and D3 sensor connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FRONT LEFT CENTER SENSOR - FRONT RIGHT CENTER SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the D3 and D4 sensor connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FRONT RIGHT CENTER SENSOR - FRONT RIGHT CORNER SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the D4 and D6 sensor connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FRONT RIGHT CORNER SENSOR - FRONT RIGHT SIDE SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the D6 and D5 sensor connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Intuitive Parking Assist System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Intuitive Parking Assist System. Result B --  REPLACE NO. 1 ULTRASONIC SENSOR (FRONT LEFT CENTER SENSOR) C --  REPLACE NO. 1 ULTRASONIC SENSOR (FRONT RIGHT CORNER SENSOR) D --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE NO. 2 ULTRASONIC SENSOR (FRONT LEFT SIDE SENSOR) (a) Replace the ultrasonic sensor    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Distance Sensor/Service and Repair/Ultrasonic Sensor (for Front Side)/Removal with a normally functioning sensor. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: APGS Sensor Communication Circuit This DTC is stored when there is an open or short circuit in the communication line between the front sensors and ECU, or when there is malfunction in a front sensor. NOTICE: - When "System initializing" is displayed on the display and navigation module display after the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) battery terminal, correct the steering angle neutral point See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Initialization. - Depending on the parts that are replaced or operations that are performed during vehicle inspection or maintenance, calibration of other systems as well as the advanced parking guidance system may be needed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Result B --  CHECK PARKING ASSIST ECU A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: Front Radar Sensor Region Code Mismatch The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor, driver monitor camera and object recognition camera sensor to detect obstacles in front of and behind the vehicle, and detect the direction that the driver's head is facing. Based on this information, the driving support ECU sends pre-collision control operation signals. When the front millimeter wave radar sensor is replaced with a new one, it acquires the country specification information from the driving support ECU. Also, when the driving support ECU is replaced with a new one, it receives country specification information from the combination meter. When country specification information in the sensor and ECU do not match, DTC C1A0A is output. If the sensor and vehicle country specification information are the same, and the combination meter and the driving support ECU country specification information are different, C1A0C is output. NOTICE: When the front millimeter wave radar sensor is replaced with a new one, adjustment of the radar sensor beam axis must be performed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Adjustments. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR (a) Enter the following menus: Powertrain / Radar Cruise /. The select "Data List". (b) Read the Data List and check the country specification information of the front millimeter wave radar sensor and vehicle. Result HINT - The country specification information of the driving support ECU is decided by the combination meter. Even if the country specification information for vehicle and sensor match, if the country specification information for the vehicle and ECU/meter do not match, it is necessary to replace the ECU and meter. - When the country specification information (North America to Europe and Europe to North America) for the vehicle and sensor do not match, the sensor's information can be overwritten by performing the sensor's axis adjustment. B --  REPLACE FRONT MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal C --  ADJUST FRONT MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Adjustments A --  REPLACE DRIVING SUPPORT ECU AND COMBINATION METER Pre-Collision System (w/ Driver Monitor Camera)

dtcs.description: Object Recognition Camera Region Code Mismatch When the object recognition ECU is replaced with a new one, it acquires the country specification information from the driving support ECU. Also, when the driving support ECU is replaced with a new one, it receives country specification information from the combination meter. When country specification information in the object recognition ECU and driver monitor ECU do not match, DTC C1A0C is output. If the sensor and vehicle country specification information are the same, and the combination meter and the driving support ECU country specification information are different, C1A0A is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Use the Data List to check if the country specification information is correct    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Lane Departure System/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures. Radar Cruise OK: Country specification information for object recognition ECU and vehicle match. NG --  REPLACE OBJECT RECOGNITION ECU OK --  REPLACE DRIVING SUPPORT ECU AND COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY Pre-Collision System (w/ Driver Monitor Camera)

dtcs.description: Driving Support ECU The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor, object recognition camera sensor and driver monitor camera to detect obstacles in front of the vehicle and the direction that the driver's head is facing. Based on this information, the driving support ECU calculates the risk of a collision and operates the pre-collision system when it determines that the risk is high. When DTC C1A01 is detected, the driving support ECU only detects the "Pre-Collision 2" DTC malfunctions. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Skid Control Buzzer Circuit Based on pre-collision control, the driving support ECU informs to the driver to be careful. The driving support ECU performs this by sending a sounding signal to the skid control ECU to sound the skid control buzzer. When the skid control ECU detects a malfunction in the skid control buzzer circuit, a malfunction signal is sent to the driving support ECU, and a DTC is output. Although the skid control ECU sends a malfunction signal to the driving support ECU, the ABS does not output a relevant malfunction detection DTC. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SKID CONTROL BUZZER) (a) Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Active Test. (b) Select "Buzzer", and perform the Active Test. OK: Skid control buzzer sounds. dtcs.description: Infrared Signal Circuit The headlight's hi beam part is equipped with a near infrared ray filter. While driving at night, when the light conditions surrounding the vehicle are darker than a certain threshold, the headlights are automatically changed to hi beam mode, and near infrared ray filter is activated. By emitting near infrared rays toward the area in front of the vehicle, the object recognition camera ability to detect obstacles is improved. HINT Even if hi beam mode is automatically set, occupants in oncoming vehicles will not be affected as the near infrared filter is activated. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HEADLIGHT ASSEMBLY (a) Start the engine. (b) Headlight (Lo/Hi) turns on. OK: Headlight normally. HINT If the near infrared ray filter is stuck in the activated position, the headlights will seems as if they are not in Hi beam mode (only a dim, red light will be emitted). If this occurs, it is necessary to replace the headlight assembly. dtcs.description: Front Radar Sensor The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor to detect obstacles in front of the vehicle. Based on this information, the driving support ECU sends pre-collision control operation signals. When the driving support ECU receives information that there is a malfunction in the front millimeter wave radar sensor, DTC C1A10 is output. This DTC is also output from the dynamic radar cruise control system. NOTICE: When the front millimeter wave radar sensor is replaced with a new one, adjustment of the front sensor beam axis must be performed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Adjustments. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE FRONT MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR (a) Replace the front millimeter wave radar sensor    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

Front Radar Sensor Beam Axis Not Adjusted The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor to detect obstacles in front of and behind the vehicle, and detect the direction that the driver's head is facing. Based on this information, the driving support ECU sends pre-collision control operation signals. When the vehicle is determined to be driving in a straight line or on a slow curve based on signals from the yaw rate sensor, etc., the front millimeter wave radar sensor assembly performs a self diagnosis to check if the sensor beam axis is deviated from the vehicle movement direction. When the front millimeter wave radar sensor assembly's beam axis is deviated, the driving support ECU detects this, and outputs DTC C1A11. After installing a new front millimeter wave radar sensor, if the sensor beam axis adjustment is not performed, DTC C1A14 is output. This DTC is also output from the dynamic radar cruise control system. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Object Recognition Camera Recognizing Distance Discrepancy When it has been determined that the beam axis of the object recognition camera has deviated, DTC C1A22 is stored. After installing a new object recognition camera, if camera beam axis adjustment is not performed, DTC C1A23 is stored. When the distance from an object detected by the left and right camera greatly differs from the distance detected by the millimeter wave radar sensor assembly, DTC C1A25 is stored. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Driver Monitor ECU The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor, driver monitor camera and object recognition camera sensor to detect obstacles in front of and behind the vehicle, and detect the direction that the driver's head is facing. Based on this information, the driving support ECU sends pre-collision control operation signals. When the driving support ECU receives information that there is a malfunction in the driver monitor ECU, DTC C1A30 is output. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Driver Monitor Camera The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor, driver monitor camera and object recognition camera sensor to detect obstacles in front of and behind the vehicle, and detect the direction that the driver's head is facing. Based on this information, the driving support ECU sends pre-collision control operation signals. When the driving support ECU receives information that there is a malfunction in the driver monitor camera, DTC C1A31 is output. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Driver Monitor Image cannot be Recognized The driving support ECU uses the front millimeter wave radar sensor, driver monitor camera and object recognition camera sensor to detect obstacles in front of and behind the vehicle, and detect the direction that the driver's head is facing. Based on this information, the driving support ECU sends pre-collision control operation signals. The driver's head position information is sent via the CAN communication line from the driver monitor camera to the driver monitor ECU, and then from the driver monitor ECU to the driving support ECU. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Brake Request Value Mismatch between ENG / EHV and Driving Support ECU The skid control ECU receives brake control request signals from the ECM and driving support ECU. When the request signals received from the ECM and driving support ECU are not consistent, the skid control ECU determines that a malfunction has occurred and sends a malfunction signal to the driving support ECU. If the driving support ECU receives a malfunction signal, DTC C1A41 is output. Although the skid control ECU sends a malfunction signal to the driving support ECU, the ABS does not output a relevant malfunction detection DTC. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Yaw Rate Sensor The skid control ECU receives vehicle stability signals from the yaw rate sensor and sends the signals to the driving support ECU and ECM. If the skid control ECU detects that the yaw rate sensor is malfunctioning, the skid control ECU sends a malfunction signal to the driving support ECU and outputs DTC C1A46. Also, when DTC C1A46 is output, the skid control ECU outputs DTC C1234 at the same time. When DTC C1A46 is detected, DTC C1234 is also output from the "ABS/VSC/TRAC". PROCEDURE

steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate sensor are output together with other DTCs for the
steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle
Start the inspection from "CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR" when using the Techstream and start from "CHECK DTC"

(d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (24 mph), turn the steering wheel to the right and left, and check that no speed sensor and yaw rate sensor DTCs are output. dtcs.description: 71 - Electric Parking Brake High Temperature If the electric parking brake is used continuously, the actuator overheat prevention stops the system. If this occurs, the parking brake indicator light (RED) blinks and the multi-information display shows the overheat warning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: ELECTRIC PARKING BRAKE OVERHEAT (a) With the overheat warning displayed, wait for approximately 1 minute. (b) Check if the overheat warning has disappeared. HINT - Check the display after turning the engine switch off and on (IG). - If the display returns to normal and the electric parking brake can be operated, clear the DTC. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: 31, C13AB/33: Open or Short in Lock Switch Circuit When electric parking brake switch is pushed to the lock side, a lock request signal is output to parking brake ECU. HINT *: When battery voltage is 11 V NOTICE: - If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. - Before disconnecting connectors or fuses, turn the engine switch off and wait 20 seconds or more. - The electric parking brake switch interior's linked double contacts may become unlinked (one is off, one is on) if the switch is halfway pushed/pulled. If this condition continues for 1 second or more, this DTC is output. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 64, C15A9/66, C15AA/66, C15AB/66: Actuator Malfunction When the VGRS system is normal, the steering actuator conducts current from the steering control ECU to the steering actuator solenoid to release the lock mechanism, enabling motor operation. The steering actuator does not operate under either of the following conditions: - The engine is off. - The motor in the actuator is locked by the lock mechanism to prevent rotation (fail-safe function). - The system is being protected from overheating (fail-safe function). If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the steering actuator lock mechanism or a motor rotation angle error, it will store these DTCs. PROCEDURE

NOTICE: Before replacing the steering actuator (power steering gear assembly), make sure to use Data List to confirm and record the "Elec Angle Position" value    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Data List / Active Test. dtcs.description: 61 - Actuator Malfunction The steering control ECU drives the actuator while the steering wheel is turned to set the gear ratio. It detects the current flowing into the actuator motor driving circuit. If the steering control ECU detects excessive current, it will store this DTC C15A1/61, turn on the master warning light, and stop VGRS operation. HINT When DTC C15A2/62 is output, a short circuit inside the steering actuator or inside the steering control ECU is suspected. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 62, C15A6/62: Actuator Malfunction The steering control ECU drives the actuator based on steering angle sensor signals and vehicle speed signals. If the steering control ECU detects excessive current flowing into the steering actuator, or an internal malfunction, it will turn on the master warning light, store DTC C15A2/62, and stop VGRS operation. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the pre driver circuit, it will turn the master warning light on, store DTC C15A6/62, and stop VGRS operation. HINT The actuator only can be checked in this procedure. If it is normal, the steering control ECU is faulty. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 63, C15C5/69: Actuator Malfunction The steering actuator drives the internal motor using the current output from the steering control ECU to change the relative angle between the tire angle and steering wheel angle. The rotation angle sensor in the steering actuator detects the motor rotation angle and outputs this information to the steering control ECU. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the rotation angle sensor circuit, it will store DTC C15A3/63. HINT *: A tire angle of 36° and a motor rotation angle of 36° correspond to the steering wheel angle. The amount of change in tire angle and motor rotation angle is from when the mode is changed to test mode. PROCEDURE

If the steering control ECU detects excessive current flowing into the steering actuator, or an internal malfunction, it will turn on the master warning light, store DTC C15A2/62, and stop VGRS operation. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the pre driver circuit, it will turn the master warning light on, store DTC C15A6/62, and stop VGRS operation. HINT The actuator only can be checked in this procedure. If it is normal, the steering control ECU is faulty. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 69 - Motor Revolution Angle Signal (Test Mode DTC) The steering actuator drives the internal motor using the current output from the steering control ECU to change the relative angle between the tire angle and steering wheel angle. The rotation angle sensor in the steering actuator detects the motor rotation angle and outputs this information to the steering control ECU. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the rotation angle sensor circuit, it will store DTC C15A3/63. HINT *: A tire angle of 36° and a motor rotation angle of 36° correspond to the steering wheel angle. The amount of change in tire angle and motor rotation angle is from when the mode is changed to test mode. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 31, C0205/32, C1271/71, C1272/72: Front Speed Sensor RH Circuit The speed sensor detects wheel speed and sends the appropriate signals to the ECU. These signals are used to control the brake control system. This speed sensor contains a sensor IC, which consists of 2 MREs (Magnetic Resistance Element). The speed sensor rotor, which consists of 48 sets of N and S poles that are arranged in a circle, is integrated with the inner race of the hub bearing. To detect the rotation direction, the output signals from the speed sensor are used to determine the relationship of the pulses that are generated by the 2 MREs. Upon receiving the speed sensor signal, the sensor IC outputs a forward or backward signal to the ECU. DTCs C1271/71 and C1272/72 are cleared when the speed sensor sends a vehicle speed signal or test mode ends. DTCs C1271/71 and C1272/72 are output only in test mode. HINT -

32 is for the front speed sensor LH. HINT Start the inspection from "CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR" when using the Techstream and start from "PERFORM TEST MODE" when not using the Techstream. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 97: Yaw Rate Sensor Malfunction The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTC U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with acceleration sensor) are output. These DTCs are also output when the calibration has not been completed. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 can be deleted when the yaw rate and acceleration sensor sends a yaw rate and/or acceleration signal or the Test Mode ends. DTCs C0371/71 and C1279/79 are output only in the Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. HINT When U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 is output together with C1234/34, C1243/43, C1244/44, C1245/45 or C1381/97, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) At a speed of 30 km/h (18 mph) or more, drive the vehicle, turn the steering wheel, and decelerate (depress the brake pedal). (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) again and check that no CAN communication system DTC is output. (e) Check that no DTCs for zero point calibration of the yaw rate sensor undone (C1210/36) and for zero point calibration of the acceleration sensor undone (C1336/98) are output. Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With C --  REPAIR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT CODE  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR INSTALLATION (a) Check that the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has been installed properly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Installation. OK: The sensor is installed to the specified torque. The sensor is not tilted. NG --  INSTALL YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR CORRECTLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (IG) (a) Disconnect the L71 sensor connector. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: Check the yaw rate and acceleration sensor signal after replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 68 - Master Reservoir Level Malfunction When a fluid level drop in the master cylinder reservoir is detected, the signal is input to the skid control ECU. When the DTC for the fluid level drop is memorized, the warning is canceled and the DTC is not stored if the fluid level returns to normal. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK BRAKE FLUID LEVEL IN RESERVOIR (a) Check that the brake fluid level is sufficient. OK: Brake fluid level is proper. HINT - If the fluid level drops, check for a fluid leak, and repair if found. - Check that the brake fluid leakage (Connection between accumulator and actuator, or actuator and wheel cylinder) - If no leak exists, add and adjust fluid and then check that the trouble code is not output again. (b) Check that there are no leaks from the connection between the accumulator pump and actuator. HINT As a visual check is very difficult, perform the check with the following procedures. (a) Bleed air from the brake actuator    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Brake Bleeding/Service and Repair. (b) Using the Techstream, check the data monitor of the accumulator pressure sensor. (c) Wait for 30 seconds without operating the brake pedal. Then check the accumulator pressure sensor output value's change is within the specified range. ABS/VSC/TRAC OK: Accumulator pressure sensor output value's change is within 0.2 V. NG --  CHECK AND REPAIR BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE AND ADD FLUID OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE FLUID LEVEL WARNING SWITCH (a) Disconnect the A18 switch connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. HINT A float is placed inside the reservoir. Its position can be changed by increasing/decreasing the level of brake fluid. Standard resistance: HINT If there is no problem after finishing the above check, adjust the brake fluid level to the MAX level. NG --  REPLACE BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER RESERVOIR ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE FLUID LEVEL WARNING SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A18 switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SKID CONTROL ECU (SWITCH INPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Reconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A18 switch connector. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. B --  END A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 95 - ECM Communication Circuit Malfunction The circuit is used to send TR(A)C and VSC control information from the skid control ECU to the ECM, and engine control information from the ECM to the skid control ECU via the CAN communication system. NOTICE: - When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of the linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. - If the ECM has been replaced, it is necessary to register the ECU See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK PART NUMBER (SKID CONTROL ECU AND ECM) (a) Check that the proper skid control ECU and ECM are installed. NG --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU AND ECM WITH PROPER ONES OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  CHECK INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS (SYMPTOMS SIMULATION)  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Check for Intermittent Problems

dtcs.description: 36, C1336/98: Zero Point Calibration of Yaw Rate Sensor Undone The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has the built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, the DTCs U0123/62 (yaw rate sensor communication trouble) and U0124/95 (acceleration sensor communication trouble) are output. The DTCs are also output when the calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of the linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT When U0123/62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 is output along with C1210/36 or C1336/98, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by
62, U0124/95 or U0126/63 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR INSTALLATION (a) Check that the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has been installed properly. OK: The sensor is tightened to the specified torque. The sensor is not tilted. NG --  INSTALL YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR CORRECTLY OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform the zero point calibration of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (24 mph) or more, and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (d) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT - *1: The DTCs are recorded because zero point calibration has not been completed. - *2: End the procedure since the same DTCs are not recorded after completion of zero point calibration. B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Replace the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform the zero point calibration of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (24 mph) or more, and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (d) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT - *1: The DTCs are recorded because zero point calibration has not been completed. - *2: End the procedure since the same DTCs are not recorded after completion of zero point calibration. B --  END A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 35 is for the front speed sensor RH. -

75-C1278/78: Foreign Object is Attached on Tip of Front Speed Sensor RH Refer to DTCs C0200/31 and C0205/32 See: C0200. DTCs C1275/75 to C1278/78 are cleared when the speed sensor sends a vehicle speed signal or test mode ends. DTCs C1275/75 to C1278/78 are output only in test mode. HINT -
36 is for the front speed sensor LH. -
38 is for the rear speed sensor RH. -

39 is for the rear speed sensor LH. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - SPEED SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Disconnect the A55 and A73 sensor connectors. (c) Disconnect the R56 and S69 sensor connectors. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: for RH for LH NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SKID CONTROL ECU (SENSOR INPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Reconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Disconnect the A55 sensor connector. (c) Disconnect the A73 sensor connector. (d) Disconnect the R56 sensor connector. (e) Disconnect the S69 sensor connector. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 20 km/h (12 mph) or more for 60 seconds or more. (d) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE FRONT AXLE HUB SUB-ASSEMBLY C --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT SPEED SENSOR TIP (a) Remove the front speed sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Removal and/or rear speed sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Speed Sensor/Removal. (b) Check the sensor tip. OK: No scratches or foreign matter on the sensor tip. NOTICE: Check the speed sensor signal after cleaning or replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. NG --  CLEAN OR REPLACE SPEED SENSOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT SPEED SENSOR ROTOR (a) Remove the front axle hub sub-assembly    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Wheel Hub/Service and Repair/Front Axle Hub/Removal. (b) Remove the rear axle hub and bearing assembly    See: Transmission and Drivetrain/Drive Axles, Bearings and Joints/Axle Shaft Assembly/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Rear Drive Shaft Assembly/Removal. (c) Check each sensor rotor. OK: No scratches, oil, or foreign matter on the rotors. HINT - The front speed sensor rotor is built into the front axle hub sub-assembly. - The rear speed sensor rotor is built into the rear axle hub and bearing assembly. - If the front and/or rear speed sensor rotor needs to be replaced, replace the front axle hub sub-assembly or rear axle hub and bearing assembly. NOTICE: Check the speed sensor signal after cleaning or replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. OK --  RECONFIRM DTC NG --  CLEAN OR REPLACE SPEED SENSOR ROTOR

REPLACE FRONT AXLE HUB SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the front axle hub sub-assembly    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Wheel Hub/Service and Repair/Front Axle Hub/Removal. HINT The front speed sensor and front speed sensor rotor are built into the front axle hub sub-assembly. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: 95 - Yaw Rate Sensor Discrimination The skid control ECU determines if the yaw rate and acceleration sensor identification information sent via CAN communication matches the yaw rate and acceleration sensor identification information memorized by the skid control ECU. NOTICE: - When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. - When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK PART NUMBER (YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR) (a) Check that the proper yaw rate and acceleration sensor is installed. OK: Proper yaw rate and acceleration sensor installed. NG --  REPLACE YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 41, C1242/42: Low Battery Positive Voltage These codes are memorized when the power source voltage for the skid control ECU drops or the voltage for the ABS MAIN1 relay and ABS MAIN2 relay operation drops. The ABS MAIN1 relay and ABS MAIN2 relay are in the skid control ECU. HINT

57: Accumulator Low Pressure The accumulator pressure sensor is built into the actuator and detects the accumulator pressure. The skid control ECU turns on the brake warning light / yellow (minor malfunction) and sounds the skid control buzzer if it senses a decrease in the accumulator pressure. HINT DTCs may be output if the accumulator pressure drops due to frequent braking (this is not a malfunction). NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT When C1202/68, C1241/41, C1252/52, C1253/53 or C1391/69 are output together with C1256/57, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by C1202/68, C1241/41, C1252/52, C1253/53 or C1391/69 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT BATTERY (a) Check the battery voltage. Standard voltage: 11 to 14 V NG --  INSPECT CHARGING SYSTEM  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check that the DTCs (electronically controlled brake system and SFI system) are recorded. Result B --  GO TO SFI SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Proceed with Troubleshooting C --  REPAIR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT CODE  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (IG1, IG2, +BI1, +BI2, +BI3, +BI4) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (IG OR BI CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (GND CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (GND CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) After 2 minutes or more have elapsed, drive the vehicle 5 km/h (3 mph) or more for 20 seconds. (d) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 62 - Deceleration Sensor The electric parking brake ECU receives vehicle tilt information from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via CAN communication. HINT *: The G sensor is judged to be stuck when the G sensor is stuck and the vehicle is driven and stopped 16 times. NOTICE: If the electric parking brake ECU or electric parking brake actuator is replaced, perform the "Reset Memory" and "ACQUIRE TENSION SENSOR ZERO POINT" procedures See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (ELECTRONICALLY CONTROLLED BRAKE SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  GO TO ELECTRONICALLY CONTROLLED BRAKE SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Parking Brake System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  GO TO ELECTRONICALLY CONTROLLED BRAKE SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System Electronically Controlled Brake System

dtcs.description: 46, C1281/81, C1364/61: Master Cylinder Pressure Sensor Malfunction The master cylinder pressure sensor and the wheel cylinder pressure sensor are built into the brake actuator. They measure the master cylinder pressure and the wheel cylinder pressure and send the data to the skid control ECU.

81 can be deleted when the master cylinder pressure sensor sends a master cylinder pressure signal or the Test Mode ends. DTC C1281/81 is output only in the Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Disconnect the A27 actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (MASTER CYLINDER PRESSURE SENSOR) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Data List mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Check the output value of the master cylinder pressure sensor on the Techstream display. OK: When the pedal is depressed, the voltage displayed on the Techstream increases. NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK PRESSURE SENSOR OPERATION (a) Check the pressure sensor operation    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Testing and Inspection/On-Vehicle Inspection. NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Perform the road and braking test. (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 47, C1346/71, C1392/48: Stroke Sensor Malfunction The stroke sensor inputs the pedal stroke into the skid control ECU.

71 can be deleted when the brake pedal stroke sensor sends a stroke sensor signal or the Test Mode ends. DTC C1346/71 is output only in the Test Mode. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake pedal stroke sensor, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NG --  ADJUST BRAKE PEDAL STROKE SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Brake Pedal Assy/Adjustments OK -- Continue to next step.
