NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE PEDAL STROKE SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the A20 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A34 sensor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT SKID CONTROL ECU (SENSOR INPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Reconnect the A20 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A34 sensor connector. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (BRAKE PEDAL STROKE SENSOR) (a) Connect the brake pedal effort gauge. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Select the Data List mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) When depressing the brake pedal with the amount of force listed in the table below, check that the output value displayed on the Techstream is normal. HINT The brake pedal must be depressed gradually. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE BRAKE PEDAL STROKE SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Braking Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Perform the road and braking test. (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 49: Open in Stop Light Switch Circuit The skid control ECU inputs the stop light switch signal and detects braking condition. The skid control ECU has a circuit for open detection. The skid control ECU outputs the DTC if it detects an open in the stop light signal input line when the stop light switch is off or an open in the stop light circuit (skid control ECU side). NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT STOP LIGHT SWITCH (a) Disconnect the stop light switch connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE STOP LIGHT SWITCH OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (STOP LIGHT SWITCH) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Data List mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Check that the stop light observed on the Techstream changes when the brake pedal is depressed. OK: When the brake pedal is depressed, the Techstream displays "ON". NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - STOP LIGHT SWITCH) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - REAR JUNCTION BLOCK ECU) (a) Disconnect the A21 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the S62 ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  GO TO LIGHTING SYSTEM  See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - STOP LIGHT SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the A21 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A35 switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start the engine. (c) Depress the brake pedal several times to test the stop light circuit. (d) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result HINT If troubleshooting has been carried out according to the "PROBLEM SYMPTOMS TABLE", refer back to the table and proceed to the next step    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Electronically Controlled Brake System/Problem Symptoms Table. B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 52, C1253/53: Brake Booster Pump Motor on Time Abnormally Long The skid control ECU detects decreases in the accumulator pressure according to the data from the accumulator pressure sensor, and then starts and stops the pump motor by operating the motor relay. The skid control ECU usually drives the ABS motor relay (ABS MTR) for electronically controlled brake system control, and the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2) for ABS control. If either is of them malfunctioning, the other will substitute. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (ABS MTR1, ABS MTR2, ABS MAIN1 AND ABS MAIN2) (a) Remove the ABS MTR1, ABS MTR2, ABS MAIN1 and ABS MAIN2 fuses from the engine room No. 2 relay block. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuses. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (ABS RELAY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. HINT - The ABS relay is a semiconductor relay. It does not emit operation sounds. - The ABS relay cannot be removed and inspected, as it is part of the engine room No. 2 relay block. (d) Remove the terminal cover. ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (BM1) (a) Disconnect the A23 pump connector. (b) Read value of the Techstream (ABS MTR relay). (1) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (2) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (3) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (ABS MTR2 RELAY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Check the operation sound of the ABS MTR2 relay and motor when operating it with the Techstream. OK: The operation sound of the ABS MTR 2 relay and motor are heard. NG --  INSPECT ABS MOTOR RELAY 2 (ABS MTR2) OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the A23 and A24 pump connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (c) Disconnect the A23 and A24 pump connectors. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ACCUMULATOR SENSOR) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Select the Data List mode on the Techstream. ABS/VSC/TRAC (d) Depress the brake pedal 4 or 5 times to operate the pump motor, and check the output value on the Techstream with the motor stopped (not braking) for 30 seconds. OK: The accumulator pressure sensor voltage change is within 0.2 V. HINT - This inspection is performed to determine if there is a long term power malfunction when low pressure is detected in the accumulator due to a sensor malfunction, fluid leak in the actuator, or accumulator deterioration. - If the reservoir fluid level drops, there may be a fluid leak. NG --  PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, AND SLRRL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the 2F junction block connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 RELAY BLOCK AND NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK (INNER CIRCUIT) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the 2B and 2F junction block connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 RELAY BLOCK AND NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (c) Disconnect the 2G junction block connector. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE ENGINE ROOM NO. 2 JUNCTION BLOCK

INSPECT ABS MOTOR RELAY 2 (ABS MTR2) (a) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE ABS MOTOR RELAY 2 (ABS MTR2)  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Brake Fluid Pump Relay/Testing and Inspection OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (NO. 3 RELAY BLOCK) (a) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP - NO. 3 RELAY BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the A24 pump connector. (b) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - NO. 3 RELAY BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the A21 ECU connector. (b) Remove the ABS motor relay 2 (ABS MTR2). (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, AND SLRRL) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream main switch on. (d) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. HINT The Active Test can be performed when the following conditions are met. - Main relay 1 and 2 are on. - Shift lever is P position. - Parking brake is on. - Vehicle speed is 0 km/h (0 mph). ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Using the Techstream, turn SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, or SLRRL ON. (f) Check that each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. OK: Each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. HINT If any of wheel cylinder's output value increases, there may be a brake fluid leak in the brake actuator. NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal

dtcs.description: 41: VGRS Control System Malfunction The skid control ECU communicates with the steering control ECU via CAN communication. If a malfunction in the VGRS system is detected, cooperative control of the steering is prohibited by the fail-safe function. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE /Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- END

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC FOR VGRS SYSTEM (a) Clear the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Tilt and Power Telescopic Steering Column System. (b) Check for the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Tilt and Power Telescopic Steering Column System. Result B --  GO TO VGRS SYSTEM  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic

dtcs.description: 66: Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires the steering angle sensor zero point every time the engine switch is turned on (IG) and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If the front wheel alignment or steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the negative battery terminal, or if the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and outputs this DTC to indicate a poor adjustment. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. HINT - When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor is erased, the steering angle sensor zero point will also be erased. - If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, the steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an off-center steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK

dtcs.description: Skid Control ECU Malfunction The skid control ECU outputs this DTC if malfunctions are found in the circuit inside the ECU by self-diagnosis. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU (a) Replace the skid control ECU    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT --  PERFORM TEST MODE (SIGNAL CHECK)  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure

dtcs.description: 11-C1314/14: Open in Main Relay 1 Circuit The ABS main relay (skid control ECU interior circuit) remains ON for approximately 2 minutes after the engine switch is turned off and the input of brake operation signals stops, and supplies power to the system to keep it ready to operate. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (ABS MAIN1, ABS MAIN2, ABS MAIN3, D-IG1-3, ECU-IG) (a) Remove the ABS MAIN1, ABS MAIN2, ABS MAIN3, D-IG1-3 and ECU-IG fuses. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuses. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TERMINAL VOLTAGE (+BI1, +BI2, +BI3, +BI4) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 31, C1316/32, C1352/21-C1359/28: SMC1 Changeover Solenoid Malfunction Each solenoid adjusts pressure which affects each wheel cylinder according to signals from the skid control ECU and controls the vehicle. The master cut solenoid (SMC 1/2) is closed and blocks the master cylinder pressure from the electronically controlled brake system control pressure when the system is normal. The master cut solenoid is open and sends the master cylinder fluid pressure to the non-assisted brake wheel cylinders during the fail-safe operation when the system is malfunctioning. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT BRAKE ACTUATOR (a) Disconnect the A27 actuator connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Disconnect the A27 actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 35: SCSS Changeover Solenoid Malfunction The stroke simulator solenoid (SCSS) generates pedal reactive effort during electronically controlled brake system control. If one or more of the front wheels loses brake booster function, the simulator operation is prohibited. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT BRAKE MASTER STROKE SIMULATOR CYLINDER ASSEMBLY (a) Disconnect the A22 cylinder connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE BRAKE MASTER STROKE SIMULATOR CYLINDER ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Hydraulic System/Brake Master Cylinder/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE MASTER STROKE SIMULATOR CYLINDER) (a) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A22 cylinder connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 62-C1344/65: Front Hydraulic System RH Malfunction The skid control ECU inputs the operating fluid pressure of each wheel cylinder using the wheel cylinder pressure sensors in order to control the braking force of each wheel. DTCs may be stored if brake fluid leaks, a wheel cylinder vibrates due to uneven wear of the brake disc rotor, or foreign matter enters the solenoid valve. DTCs may be stored if the line pressure drops during air bleeding or brake pad is replaced. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. HINT When C1364/61 is output together with C1341/62, C1342/63, C1343/64 and C1344/65, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by C1364/61 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE (a) Check that there is no fluid leakage in the brake line between the brake actuator and the wheel cylinder which is the cause of DTCs. (b) Check for a brake fluid leak from the connection between the accumulator pump and brake actuator    See: C1202. (c) Check that the brake is not dragging. OK: There is no fluid leakage or dragging. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE APPLICABLE PART OK -- Continue to next step.
PERFORM AIR BLEED (a) Bleed the air from the front and rear brake systems    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Brake Bleeding/Service and Repair. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (BRAKE ACTUATOR - BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the A27 actuator connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK PRESSURE SENSOR OPERATION   See: C1246 NG --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Repeat the braking test under malfunction conditions recreated based on the "FREEZE FRAME DATA"    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Freeze Frame Data. (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 66, C1368/67: Linear Solenoid Valve Offset Learning Undone The skid control ECU stores and corrects the difference in each individual part such as the stroke sensor, actuator solenoids. Perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration if these parts are replaced. The DTCs are canceled when the result of linear valve offset learning is normal. NOTICE: When replacing the brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check that the DTCs (electronically controlled brake system and SFI system) are recorded. Result B --  GO TO SFI SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Proceed with Troubleshooting C --  CHECK FOR CIRCUIT INDICATED BY OUTPUT CODE  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION OF LINEAR SOLENOID VALVE AND CALIBRATION (a) Perform initialization of the linear solenoid valve and calibration    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTCs are recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 54 - Accumulator Pressure Sensor Malfunction The accumulator pressure sensor is built into the brake actuator. The skid control ECU detects the accumulator pressure based on the data sent from the accumulator pressure sensor, and then runs and stops the pump motor by operating the motor relay. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE ACTUATOR) (a) Disconnect the A19 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A27 actuator connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FREEZE FRAME DATA (a) Check the information code from the "FREEZE FRAME DATA" memorized when the DTC (C1365/54) is stored    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Freeze Frame Data. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 43 - Capacitor Malfunction The brake control power supply (capacitor) is used as auxiliary power for brake control when battery voltage is low. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FREEZE FRAME DATA (a) Check the information code from the "FREEZE FRAME DATA" memorized when the DTC (C1377/43) is stored    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Freeze Frame Data. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Service and Repair/Removal C --  INSPECT BATTERY A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FUSE (ABS MAIN4) (a) Remove the ABS MAIN4 fuse from the luggage room relay block (junction block). (b) Measure the resistance of the fuse. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY (+BC AND GND TERMINAL) (a) Disconnect the S49 power supply connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (+BC OR GND CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY (OUT1 AND OUT2 TERMINAL) (a) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. HINT Perform inspection while the brake is not operated. Standard voltage: OK --  RECONFIRM DTC NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Disconnect the S49 power supply connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: OK --  RECONFIRM DTC NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR

INSPECT BATTERY (a) Check the battery voltage. Standard voltage: 11 to 14 V NG --  INSPECT CHARGING SYSTEM  See: Starting and Charging/Charging System/Testing and Inspection OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY (+BC TERMINAL) (a) Disconnect the S49 power supply connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (+BC CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensors, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 44 - Capacitor Communication Malfunction Refer to DTC C1377/43 See: C1377. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY (a) Disconnect the S49 power supply connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (IG1, GND CIRCUIT) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SKID CONTROL ECU - BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY) (a) Disconnect the A19 and A21 ECU connectors. (b) Disconnect the S49 power supply connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Check if the same DTC is recorded    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT Reinstall the sensor, connectors, etc. and restore the vehicle to its prior condition before rechecking for DTCs. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FREEZE FRAME DATA (a) Check the information code from the "FREEZE FRAME DATA" memorized when the DTC (C1378/44) is stored    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Freeze Frame Data. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE CONTROL POWER SUPPLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Service and Repair/Removal A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 69 - Accumulator Leak Malfunction The DTC is stored if a brake fluid, internal or other leak is detected due to improper sealing in the brake actuator or brake accumulator pump. Internal leakage is suspected if the pump motor operates frequently without braking. NOTICE: When replacing the skid control ECU or brake actuator, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK BRAKE FLUID LEAKAGE (a) Check that there is no fluid leakage in the brake line between the brake actuator and the wheel cylinder which is the cause of DTCs. (b) Check that the brake is not dragging. OK: There is no fluid leakage or dragging. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE APPLICABLE PART OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch OFF. (c) Turn the engine switch ON (IG). (d) Check if the same DTC C1391 is recorded. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, AND SLRRL) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch ON (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream main switch on. (d) Select the Active Test mode on the Techstream. HINT The Active Test can be performed when the following conditions are met. - Main relay 1 and 2 are on. - Shift lever is P position. - Parking brake is on. - Vehicle speed is 0 km/h (0 mph). ABS/VSC/TRAC (e) Using the Techstream, turn SMC1, SMC2, SLRRR, or SLRRL ON. (f) Check that each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. OK: Each wheel cylinder's output value does not increase. HINT If any of wheel cylinder's output value increases, there may be a brake fluid leak in the brake actuator. Result B --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE BRAKE ACTUATOR Replace the brake actuator    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. NEXT --  REPLACE BRAKE ACCUMULATOR PUMP  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Hydraulic Control Assembly - Antilock Brakes/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal

dtcs.description: 65 - Lost Communication with Engine ECU The skid control ECU receives signals from the ECM via the CAN communication system. PROCEDURE s/SFI System

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (SFI SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT --  GO TO ENGINE CONTROL SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

dtcs.description: 66, C1445/66: Steering Angle Sensor Initialization Incomplete The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the engine switch is turned on (IG) and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. NOTICE: - When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. - Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the output DTC is related to temporary loss of the steering angle zero point calibration, such as when the power voltage drops when the battery is removed, and confirm if this condition occurred. HINT - When DTC U0073/94, U0100/65, U0123/62, U0124/95, U0126/63, U0128/13 and/or U0130/68 is output together with DTC

63, U0128/13 and/or U0130/68 first See: U Code Charts/U0073/Electronically Controlled Brake System. - When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be output even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and acceleration sensor are output together with other DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and acceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and check that no CAN communication system DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (22 mph) and turn the steering wheel to the right and left and check that no speed sensor and yaw rate and deceleration sensor DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT - If there is a malfunction in the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, an abnormal value may be output although the steering angle sensor is normal. - If the speed sensor and the yaw rate and acceleration sensor DTCs are output simultaneously, repair the sensors and inspect the steering angle sensor. - If the same DTC C1439/66 and/or C1445/66 is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops. B --  INSPECT CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With C --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System D --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If the front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an off-centered position of the steering wheel, acquire yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Start the engine. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 35 km/h (22 mph) or more and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (e) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If the same DTC C1439/66 and/or C1445/66 is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops. B --  END A --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 98 - Steering Angle Sensor Unusual Bank Angle Detected The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition. If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123/62 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124/95 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are output. NOTICE: - When replacing the skid control ECU, perform initialization of linear solenoid valve and calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. - Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the vehicle driving record shows that the vehicle was driven on a bank at an angle of 30°, and confirm if this condition occurred. HINT When DTC U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U126/63 is output together with DTC C1440/98, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by DTC U0123/62, U0124/95 and/or U0126/63 first See: U Code Charts/U0073/Electronically Controlled Brake System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and check that no CAN communication system DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 30 km/h (19 mph) or more, turn the steering wheel, and decelerate (depress the brake pedal) the vehicle and check that no yaw rate and acceleration sensor DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT

CHECK YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR INSTALLATION (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Check that the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has been installed properly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. OK: The sensor is tightened to the specified torque. The sensor is not installed in a tilted position. NG --  INSTALL YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR CORRECTLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Installation OK --  REPLACE SKID CONTROL ECU  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Electronic Brake Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 44, C1443/34: Invalid Data Received from Deceleration Sensor The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Start and idle the engine for 60 seconds, and then stop the engine. (c) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: POWER STEERING: POWER STEERING SYSTEM: C1511-C1513: Torque Sensor Circuit Malfunction The torque sensor converts rotation torque input to the steering wheel into an electrical signal and sends it to the ECU. Based on this signal, the ECU detects steering effort. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Check the connection of the power steering ECU and rack and pinion power steering gear connectors. (b) Visually inspect the terminals of the power steering ECU and rack and pinion power steering gear connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (TORQUE SENSOR) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: If replacing the power steering gear assembly, clear the rotation angle sensor calibration value, initialize the rotation angle sensor value, and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. Result: B --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/ VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal C --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/o VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/o VGRS)/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU (a) Replace the power steering ECU    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

INITIALIZE ROTATION ANGLE SENSOR AND CALIBRATE TORQUE SENSOR ZERO POINT (a) Initialize the rotation angle sensor value and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Steering System. Result: NOTICE: If replacing the power steering gear assembly, clear the rotation angle sensor calibration value, initialize the rotation angle sensor value, and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. B --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/ VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal C --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/o VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/o VGRS)/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: Torque Sensor Zero Point Adjustment Undone - These DTCs do not indicate a malfunction. The power steering ECU stores these DTCs (C1515 or C1525) when it determines that motor rotation angle sensor value initialization and torque sensor zero point calibration have not been performed. When the IG terminal voltage is 9 V or less, motor rotation angle sensor initialization and torque sensor zero point calibration cannot be performed. - These DTCs do not indicate a malfunction. The power steering ECU stores these DTCs (C1516 or C1526) when it determines that motor rotation angle sensor value initialization and torque sensor zero point calibration are incomplete. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RECONFIRM DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Steering System. dtcs.description: Vehicle Speed Signal Malfunction The power steering ECU receives vehicle speed signals from the skid control ECU via CAN communication. The ECU provides appropriate assisting force in accordance with the vehicle speed, based on the signals. If the ECU detects this DTC, it adjusts the amount of power assistance for a speed of 100 km/h (62 mph) and continues the system control. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: PIG Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The power steering converter assembly boosts the PIG power supply battery voltage from 12 V to approximately 46 V. This voltage is then supplied to the power steering ECU to operate the motors. As a fail-safe function, when the PIG power supply and power steering converter assembly are malfunctioning, power assistance stops. PROCEDURE

Result: B --  CONNECT CONNECTORS CORRECTLY C --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal D --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/ VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal E --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/o VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/o VGRS)/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER STEERING ECU - POWER STEERING MOTOR) (a) Disconnect the C1 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the C3 motor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (POWER STEERING MOTOR) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: - If replacing the power steering gear assembly, clear the rotation angle sensor value calibration value, initialize the rotation angle sensor value, and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. - If replacing the power steering ECU, initialize the rotation angle sensor value and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. Result: B --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/ VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal C --  REPLACE POWER STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY (w/o VGRS)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/o VGRS)/Removal A --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: DC / DC Converter IG Power Supply Voltage The power steering ECU detects malfunctions in the power steering converter's IG power supply voltage based on the power steering converter's WDD communication signal (DC / DC condition notification signal). As a fail-safe function, when the IG power supply and power steering converter are malfunctioning, power assistance stops. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK POWER STEERING CONVERTER ASSEMBLY (IG TERMINAL) (a) Disconnect the A46 converter connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING CONVERTER ASSEMBLY  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with WDD Signal of DC / DC Converter Based on the WDD signal (DC / DC condition notification signal) from the power steering converter, the power steering ECU processes and outputs signals. Based on the LDD signal (EPS demand signal) from the power steering ECU, the power steering converter processes and outputs signals. As a fail-safe function, when the system is malfunctioning, power assistance is limited. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER STEERING ECU - POWER STEERING CONVERTER ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the A44 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the A46 converter connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (BATTERY - POWER STEERING CONVERTER ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the A46 converter connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.
REPLACE POWER STEERING CONVERTER ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the power steering converter assembly    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Steering System. Result: NOTICE: If replacing the power steering ECU, initialize the rotation angle sensor value and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. B --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: Lost Communication between EPS ECU and VGRS ECU Based on the steering angle sensor signal, the power steering ECU determines if the correct type of steering angle sensor is installed. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR (a) Check if a steering angle sensor that complies with the vehicle's specifications is installed. OK: Steering angle sensor that complies with vehicle's specifications is installed. NG --  REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Steering Angle Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: DC-DC Converter Malfunction As a fail-safe function, when the power steering converter assembly is malfunctioning, power assistance stops. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK POWER STEERING ECU (PIG TERMINAL) (a) Disconnect the A44 ECU connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NOTICE: If replacing the power steering ECU, initialize the rotation angle sensor value and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CONVERTER - BATTERY AND ECU) (a) Disconnect the A47 converter connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (c) Disconnect the A44 ECU connector. (d) Disconnect the A46 converter connector. (e) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CONVERTER - BATTERY) (a) Disconnect the A46 converter connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Power Steering System. Result: B --  GO TO DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Power Steering System A --  REPLACE POWER STEERING CONVERTER ASSEMBLY  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Battery Power Supply Voltage Malfunction As a fail-safe function, when the battery power supply voltage is malfunctioning, power assistance stops. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT FUSE (EPS ECU) (a) Remove the EPS ECU fuse from the engine room No. 2 relay block. (b) Measure the resistance of the fuse. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FUSE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER STEERING ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the A44 ECU connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: If replacing the power steering ECU, initialize the rotation angle sensor value and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Assist Map Number Un-Writing This DTC is output when one of the following occurs: 1) the power steering ECU selects assist map according to the vehicle specifications from the suspension control ECU, but the assist map is not written in the power steering ECU, or 2) the power steering ECU determines that the vehicle specifications and the assist map are inconsistent. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ASSIST MAP WRITING (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for 30 seconds or more. HINT The assist map is automatically written when approximately 30 seconds have elapsed after the engine switch is turned on (IG). DTC

CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs and confirm that there are no problems in the CAN communication system    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System. Result: B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU (a) Check if a suspension control ECU that complies with the vehicle's specifications is installed. OK: Suspension control ECU that complies with vehicle's specifications is installed. NOTICE: If replacing the power steering ECU, initialize the rotation angle sensor value and calibrate the torque sensor zero point    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Steering System. NG --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE POWER STEERING ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 51 - Actuator Neutral Position Calibration Undone If the steering control ECU or steering actuator is replaced, or if the tie-rod ends are adjusted, VGRS system calibration is required. If the steering control ECU detects that steering angle adjustment is incomplete, it will store DTC C1591/51. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. OK:

PERFORM VGRS SYSTEM CALIBRATION (a) Perform VGRS system calibration    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. Result: B --  GO TO DTC C1592/52  See: C1592 C --  GO TO DTC C1593/53  See: C1593 A --  END

dtcs.description: 52 - Actuator Neutral Position Calibration Incomplete The steering control ECU first checks the actuator angle neutral position calibration flag after entering test mode. If the ECU detects that steering actuator angle neutral position calibration is incomplete twice, it will store DTC C1592/52. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM VGRS SYSTEM CALIBRATION (a) Perform VGRS system calibration    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. Result: B --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal C --  CLEAR DTC D --  REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR (POWER STEERING LINK ASSEMBLY)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 53 - Actuator Standard Position Malfunction If the steering control ECU detects a steering actuator standard position error, it will store DTC C1593/53. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Variable Gear Ratio Steering System. Result: NG --  REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR (POWER STEERING LINK ASSEMBLY)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Gear/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Gear (w/ VGRS)/Removal OK --  END

dtcs.description: ECU Malfunction When the self-diagnosis determines of the parking assist ECU that the ECU has a circuit malfunction, this DTC is output. NOTICE: - When "System initializing" is displayed on the display and navigation module display after the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) battery terminal, correct the steering angle neutral point See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Initialization. - Depending on the parts that are replaced or operations that are performed during vehicle inspection or maintenance, calibration of other systems as well as the advanced parking guidance system may be needed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Use the Data List to check if the parking assist ECU is functioning properly    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Advanced Parking Guidance System Result B --  REPLACE PARKING ASSIST ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Parking Assist ECU (w/ Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

ADJUST SETTING (PARKING ASSIST ECU INITIALIZATION) HINT - The parking assist ECU stores different types of information during initialization. If the parking assist ECU cannot read the stored information when it is activated, the parking assist ECU judges that there is an internal malfunction and stores this DTC. - If the parking assist ECU cannot read the stored information when it is activated, allowing the ECU to store the information again may return the system to normal. (a) Use the transition of diagnosis screens (note which transition pattern occurs) to confirm if the parking assist ECU was able to properly store the information from initialization    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Diagnosis System. HINT The procedure to make the parking assist ECU store the vehicle information differs when the parking assist ECU has stored the information (initialized) and when the information has not been stored (not initialized). (b) Perform the following procedure depending on the memory conditions to register the vehicle information into the parking assist ECU. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Result HINT - If DTC C1611 is output, perform the procedure to allow the parking assist ECU to store the vehicle information again. - If DTC C1611 is still output after performing the procedure to make the parking assist ECU store the vehicle information 3 times, replace the parking assist ECU. - If DTC C1611 is stored frequently, replace the parking assist ECU even if the DTC is not output when rechecking for DTCs. B --  REPLACE PARKING ASSIST ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Parking Assist ECU (w/ Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: IG Voltage is Low or High When the self-diagnosis of the parking assist ECU determines that the input IG voltage is abnormal, this DTC is output. NOTICE: - When "System initializing" is displayed on the display and navigation module display after the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) battery terminal, correct the steering angle neutral point See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Initialization. - Depending on the parts that are replaced or operations that are performed during vehicle inspection or maintenance, calibration of other systems as well as the advanced parking guidance system may be needed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. - Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Failure When the self-diagnosis of the parking assist ECU determines that the steering angle sensor is malfunction, this DTC is output. NOTICE: - When "System initializing" is displayed on the display and navigation module display after the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) battery terminal, correct the steering angle neutral point See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Initialization. - Depending on the parts that are replaced or operations that are performed during vehicle inspection or maintenance, calibration of other systems as well as the advanced parking guidance system may be needed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Result B --  REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

REPLACE STEERING ANGLE SENSOR (a) Replace the steering angle sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Steering Angle Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Result B --  REPLACE PARKING ASSIST ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Parking Assist ECU (w/ Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal A --  END (STEERING ANGLE SENSOR IS DEFECTIVE)

dtcs.description: Engine/Hybrid System Malfunction When the self-diagnosis of the parking assist ECU determines that the SFI system (connected through the ECM via CAN communication) is malfunctioning, this DTC is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Result HINT - The DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in the SFI system. - If troubleshooting and repair of the malfunction in the SFI system has been completed, DTC C1640 will not be output again when rechecking for DTCs. - If DTC C1640 is stored frequently, duplicate the problem symptoms and perform the inspection again even if the DTC is not output when rechecking for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. B --  GO TO SFI SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Service Precautions A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: Ambient Temperature High When the self-diagnosis of the parking assist ECU determines that the ambient temperature has increased to a level that may cause damage to ECU functions, this DTC is output. NOTICE: - When "System initializing" is displayed on the display and navigation module display after the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) battery terminal, correct the steering angle neutral point See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Initialization. - Depending on the parts that are replaced or operations that are performed during vehicle inspection or maintenance, calibration of other systems as well as the advanced parking guidance system may be needed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. - Maintain the cabin temperature at ambient temperature, and wait until the parking assist ECU temperature is at the ambient temperature. Then perform the inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System.
