Result HINT - If the DTC is not output again when rechecking for DTCs, it may have been stored due to a temporary increase in ambient temperature. - If DTC C1662 is stored frequently, duplicate the problem symptoms and perform the inspection again even if the DTC is not output when rechecking for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting. B --  REPLACE PARKING ASSIST ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Parking Assist ECU (w/ Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: Back Camera Initialization Incomplete When the self-diagnosis of the parking assist ECU determines that the television camera assembly optical axis adjustment is incomplete, this DTC is output. NOTICE: - When "System initializing" is displayed on the display and navigation module display after the cable is disconnected from the negative (-) battery terminal, correct the steering angle neutral point See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Initialization. - Depending on the parts that are replaced or operations that are performed during vehicle inspection or maintenance, calibration of other systems as well as the advanced parking guidance system may be needed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM CAMERA OPTICAL AXIS ADJUSTMENT (a) Perform the following procedure depending on the memory conditions to register the vehicle information into the parking assist ECU. HINT Before performing the television camera optical axis adjustment, the height control sensor vehicle height difference setting must be performed on the "HEIGHT SET" screen. (1) Perform the height control sensor vehicle height difference setting    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. (2) Perform the television camera assembly optical axis adjustment    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

(b) Check for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Advanced Parking Guidance System. Result HINT - If DTC C1691 is output, perform the television camera assembly optical axis adjustment again. - If DTC C1691 is still output after performing the television camera assembly optical axis adjustment 3 times, replace the parking assist ECU. B --  REPLACE PARKING ASSIST ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Parking Assist ECU (w/ Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

B --  REPLACE PARKING ASSIST ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Parking Assist Control Module/Service and Repair/Parking Assist ECU (w/ Advanced Parking Guidance System)/Removal A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: Wheel Base Information Discrepancy When the wheelbase information in the EEPROM of the parking assist ECU and the wheelbase information received from the AFS ECU via CAN communication do not match, this DTC is output. If a parking assist ECU or AFS ECU is installed into a vehicle with a different wheelbase, this DTC is output and "System Initializing" is displayed. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK WHEEL BASE INFORMATION (a) Write the AFS ECU's wheelbase information (CAN information) to the EEPROM of the park assist ECU. (1) Enter the SIGNAL CHECK screen on the Diagnosis Menu    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Diagnosis System. (2) Select the "NEXT" or "OK" button until "STEERING ANGLE SETTING" screen appears. (3) Select the "OK" button on the "STEERING ANGLE SETTING" screen. (b) On the "SIGNAL CHECK" screen, check if the body type is properly displayed    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Diagnosis System. (c) Select the "NEXT" button on the "VERIFY MODE" screen to update the information to the ECU    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Advanced Parking Guidance System/Calibration. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: AIR SUSPENSION SYSTEM: C1711-C1714: Front Height Control Sensor RH Circuit Malfunction The height control sensor changes its resistance value based on changes in vehicle height. The suspension control ECU outputs a fixed voltage of 5 V to the height control sensor's SHB terminal. If the sensor's resistance value changes, the sensor's voltage value changes accordingly. The suspension control ECU then inputs the voltage value from the sensor to detect the vehicle height change. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If DTC C1782 (Power Source Voltage Malfunction) is output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for C1782 first See: C1782. - Before replacing the suspension control ECU, perform all of the following again: 1) symptom simulation See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure; 2) DTC inspection; and 3) Techstream inspection (Data List or Active Test). If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR (a) Check the front height control sensor: (1) Remove the front height control sensor RH or front height control sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front LH)/Removal. NOTICE: Do not drop the height control sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (2) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (3) Connect the positive (+) end of the battery to terminal 3 (SHB) of the height control sensor and the negative (-) end of the battery to terminal 1 (SHG). While applying 4.5 V, slowly moving the sensor's link up and down, and measure the voltage between terminal 2 (SHFR*1 or SHFL*2) and terminal 1 (SHG). HINT *1: for RH *2: for LH Standard Voltage: for RH for LH NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of more than 6 V. (b) Check the rear height control sensor: (1) Remove the rear height control sensor RH or rear height control sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear LH)/Removal. NOTICE: Do not drop the height control sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (2) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (3) Connect the positive (+) end of the battery to terminal 3 (SHB) of the height control sensor and the negative (-) end of the battery to terminal 1 (SHG). While applying 4.5 V, slowly moving the sensor's link up and down, and measure the voltage between terminal 2 (SHRR*1 or SHRL*2) and terminal 1 (SHG). HINT *1: for RH *2: for LH Standard Voltage: for RH for LH NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of more than 6 V. Result B --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR RH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front LH)/Removal C --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front LH)/Removal D --  REPLACE REAR HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR RH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear LH)/Removal E --  REPLACE REAR HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear LH)/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR - SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU) (a) Check the front height control sensor RH side: (C1711) (1) Turn the engine switch off. (2) Disconnect the A39 and L88 ECU connectors. (3) Disconnect the A54 sensor connector. (4) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (b) Check the front height control sensor LH side: (C1712) (1) Turn the engine switch off. (2) Disconnect the A39 and L88 ECU connectors. (3) Disconnect the A4 sensor connector. (4) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (c) Check the rear height control sensor RH side: (C1713) (1) Turn the engine switch off. (2) Disconnect the R1 ECU connector. (3) Disconnect the "A" sensor connector. (4) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Check the rear height control sensor LH side: (C1714) (1) Turn the engine switch off. (2) Disconnect the R1 ECU connector. (3) Disconnect the "B" sensor connector. (4) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: AIR SUSPENSION SYSTEM: C1711-C1714: Front Height Control Sensor RH Circuit Malfunction The height control sensor changes its resistance value based on changes in vehicle height. The suspension control ECU outputs a fixed voltage of 5 V to the height control sensor's SHB terminal. If the sensor's resistance value changes, the sensor's voltage value changes accordingly. The suspension control ECU then inputs the voltage value from the sensor to detect the vehicle height change. If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR (a) Check the front height control sensor: (1) Remove the front height control sensor RH or front height control sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front LH)/Removal. NOTICE: Do not drop the height control sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (2) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (3) Connect the positive (+) end of the battery to terminal 3 (SHB) of the height control sensor and the negative (-) end of the battery to terminal 1 (SHG). While applying 4.5 V, slowly moving the sensor's link up and down, and measure the voltage between terminal 2 (SHFR*1 or SHFL*2) and terminal 1 (SHG). HINT *1: for RH *2: for LH Standard Voltage: for RH for LH NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of more than 6 V. (b) Check the rear height control sensor: (1) Remove the rear height control sensor RH or rear height control sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear LH)/Removal. HINT *1: for RH *2: for LH Standard Voltage: for RH for LH NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of more than 6 V. Result B --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR RH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front LH)/Removal C --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front LH)/Removal D --  REPLACE REAR HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR RH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear LH)/Removal E --  REPLACE REAR HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear LH)/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR - SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU) (a) Check the front height control sensor RH side: (C1711) (1) Turn the engine switch off. (2) Disconnect the A39 and L88 ECU connectors. (3) Disconnect the A54 sensor connector. (4) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (b) Check the front height control sensor LH side: (C1712) (1) Turn the engine switch off. (2) Disconnect the A39 and L88 ECU connectors. (3) Disconnect the A4 sensor connector. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: AIR SUSPENSION SYSTEM: C1715-C1717, C1796-C1798: Front Acceleration Sensor RH Malfunction The acceleration sensor (up & down G sensor) detects the upward and downward acceleration of the vehicle, and outputs it as a voltage to the suspension control ECU. Up & down G sensors are installed in 3 locations: 1) the suspension control ECU, 2) the driver side instrument panel, and 3) the rear quarter. Each up & down G sensor independently detects the upward and downward acceleration. During a test mode inspection, the suspension control ECU reads the fluctuations in each sensor's signal. If a sensor's signal does not fluctuate, test mode DTCs C1796, C1797 and C1798 are output. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If DTC C1782 (Power Source Voltage Malfunction) is output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for C1782 first See: C1782. - Before replacing the suspension control ECU, perform all of the following again: 1) symptom simulation See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure; 2) DTC inspection; and 3) Techstream inspection (Data List or Active Test). If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. - If DTCs C1715 and C1796 (Front Acceleration Sensor RH Malfunction) are output, replace the suspension control ECU (built into front acceleration sensor RH). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK ACCELERATION SENSOR (DATA LIST) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menu item: Chassis / Air suspension / Data List. (e) According to the display on the Techstream, read the Data List. Air suspension NG --  INSPECT ACCELERATION SENSOR (FRONT LH OR REAR) OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Perform a road test. (c) Check for DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT ACCELERATION SENSOR (FRONT LH OR REAR) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Remove the front acceleration sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Acceleration Sensor/Removal or rear acceleration sensor    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Acceleration Sensor/Removal. NOTICE: - Do not drop the acceleration sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (c) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (d) Connect the positive (+) end of the battery to terminal 3 (SGB*1, SGV*2) of the acceleration sensor and the negative (-) end of the battery to terminal 2 (SGG*1, SGND*2). Then measure the voltage between terminal 1 (SGFL*1, SGRR*2) and terminal 2 (SGG*1, SGND*2) HINT *1: for Front LH *2: for Rear NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of more than 6 V. HINT When the acceleration sensor is tilted, it may output a different value. Standard Voltage: for Front LH: (C1716, C1797) for Rear: (C1717, C1798) Result B --  REPLACE FRONT ACCELERATION SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Acceleration Sensor/Removal C --  REPLACE REAR ACCELERATION SENSOR  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Acceleration Sensor/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ACCELERATION SENSOR - SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU) (a) Check the front acceleration sensor LH side: (C1716, C1797) (1) Disconnect the R1 ECU connector. (2) Disconnect the L17 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (b) Check the rear acceleration sensor side: (C1717, C1798) (1) Disconnect the R1 ECU connector. (2) Disconnect the R26 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

NOTICE: - Do not drop the acceleration sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (c) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. HINT When the acceleration sensor is tilted, it may output a different value. dtcs.description: Exhaust Solenoid Valve Circuit Malfunction The exhaust valve is installed onto the height control compressor. Based on signals from the suspension control ECU, the exhaust valve opens the exhaust solenoid valve, which releases the high pressure air within the system out of the vehicle. The exhaust valve operates until the target vehicle height is achieved. When achieved, the height control sensor outputs a signal to the suspension control ECU to stop the operation of the exhaust valve. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If DTC C1782 (Power Source Voltage Malfunction) is output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for C1782 first See: C1782. - Before replacing the suspension control ECU, perform all of the following again: 1) symptom simulation See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure; 2) DTC inspection; and 3) Techstream inspection (Data List or Active Test). If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Height Control Compressor Motor Circuit Malfunction The signal from the suspension control ECU turns on the suspension control relay. At that time, battery voltage is input to the height control compressor motor through the suspension control relay. The height control compressor motor starts. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - Before replacing the suspension control ECU, perform all of the following again: 1) symptom simulation See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure; 2) DTC inspection; and 3) Techstream inspection (Data List or Active Test). If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: INSPECT SUSPENSION CONTROL RELAY (AIR SUS) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Remove the suspension control relay from the engine room No. 1 relay block. (b) Connect terminal 1 (B) to the battery positive (+) terminal, and terminal 2 (E) to the battery negative (-) terminal. (c) Check the operating of the compressor motor. OK: Motor operates NOTICE: - Do not operate the height control compressor for 60 seconds or more. - Since a short and a lock-up inside the height control compressor cause excessive current to flow, stop operation immediately when it does not rotate. NG --  REPLACE HEIGHT CONTROL COMPRESSOR  See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Compressor/Pump/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Continuous Electric Current to Height Control Compressor Motor The signal from the suspension control ECU operates the suspension control relay and the height control compressor motor starts. The height control compressor motor operates until the targeted vehicle height is reached. Then the height control sensor sends the signal to the suspension control ECU, and stops the height control compressor motor. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - Before replacing the suspension control ECU, perform all of the following again: 1) symptom simulation See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure; 2) DTC inspection; and 3) Techstream inspection (Data List or Active Test). If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. - The system may output DTC C1761 even when the system is normal if one of the following occurs: 1) the vehicle is full of passengers and the luggage compartment is full; 2) the vehicle is being driven or is stopped on an irregular surface; or 3) the air ducts are frozen. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

NG --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL VALVE  See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Ride Control Solenoid Valve/Service and Repair/Control Valve Assembly (for Front Side)/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

steps.instruction: CHECK VEHICLE IDENTIFICATION INFORMATION STATUS (a) Check that vehicle identification information has been stored    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. Result B --  PERFORM VEHICLE IDENTIFICATION INFORMATION REGISTRATION  See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK PART NUMBER (a) Check that the part numbers of the installed steering control ECU, ECM and AFS ECU are correct. NOTICE: When removing the steering control ECU, ECM and/or AFS ECU to check the part number, or when replacing the steering control ECU, ECM and/or AFS ECU, be sure to follow the appropriate procedures and precautions    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. OK: Steering control ECU, ECM and AFS ECU with correct part numbers are installed. NG --  REPLACE CORRESPONDING ECU OK -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check if DTC C1789 is output    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: AIR SUSPENSION SYSTEM: C1715-C1717, C1796-C1798: Front Acceleration Sensor RH Malfunction The acceleration sensor (up & down G sensor) detects the upward and downward acceleration of the vehicle, and outputs it as a voltage to the suspension control ECU. Up & down G sensors are installed in 3 locations: 1) the suspension control ECU, 2) the driver side instrument panel, and 3) the rear quarter. Each up & down G sensor independently detects the upward and downward acceleration. During a test mode inspection, the suspension control ECU reads the fluctuations in each sensor's signal. If a sensor's signal does not fluctuate, test mode DTCs C1796, C1797 and C1798 are output. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If DTC C1782 (Power Source Voltage Malfunction) is output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for C1782 first See: C1782. - Before replacing the suspension control ECU, perform all of the following again: 1) symptom simulation See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure; 2) DTC inspection; and 3) Techstream inspection (Data List or Active Test). If no malfunctions are found in other areas, replace the suspension control ECU. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. - If DTCs C1715 and C1796 (Front Acceleration Sensor RH Malfunction) are output, replace the suspension control ECU (built into front acceleration sensor RH). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK ACCELERATION SENSOR (DATA LIST) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menu item: Chassis / Air suspension / Data List. (e) According to the display on the Techstream, read the Data List. Air suspension NG --  INSPECT ACCELERATION SENSOR (FRONT LH OR REAR) OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Perform a road test. (c) Check for DTC    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT ACCELERATION SENSOR (FRONT LH OR REAR) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Remove the front acceleration sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Acceleration Sensor/Removal or rear acceleration sensor    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Acceleration Sensor/Removal. NOTICE: - Do not drop the acceleration sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (c) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (d) Connect the positive (+) end of the battery to terminal 3 (SGB*1, SGV*2) of the acceleration sensor and the negative (-) end of the battery to terminal 2 (SGG*1, SGND*2). Then measure the voltage between terminal 1 (SGFL*1, SGRR*2) and terminal 2 (SGG*1, SGND*2) HINT *1: for Front LH *2: for Rear NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of more than 6 V. HINT When the acceleration sensor is tilted, it may output a different value. Standard Voltage: for Front LH: (C1716, C1797) for Rear: (C1717, C1798) Result B --  REPLACE FRONT ACCELERATION SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Acceleration Sensor/Removal C --  REPLACE REAR ACCELERATION SENSOR  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Rear Acceleration Sensor/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ACCELERATION SENSOR - SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU) (a) Check the front acceleration sensor LH side: (C1716, C1797) (1) Disconnect the R1 ECU connector. (2) Disconnect the L17 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (b) Check the rear acceleration sensor side: (C1717, C1798) (1) Disconnect the R1 ECU connector. (2) Disconnect the R26 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 11-C2115/15: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. (f) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (g) Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed. OK: Each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen changes to the value of the tire pressure. NOTICE: - It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. - When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270° (including the spare tire). Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. (h) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed, set the tire pressures to the appropriate specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has not changed after rechecking, inspect the other problem (malfunction in transmission or reception function)    See: C2121. NG --  GO TO TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER INSPECTION PROCEDURE  See: C2121 OK --  END

dtcs.description: 21-C2125/25, C2181/81-C2185/85: No Signal from Transmitter ID1 in Main Mode The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ OUTPUT DTC (DTC C2179/79) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Trouble Codes / (e) Read DTCs. Result NOTICE: When DTC C2179/79 is output, troubleshoot that DTC first. Then troubleshoot DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25. B --  GO TO DTC C2179/79  See: C2179 A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check for the conditions described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may turn on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located around or in the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings, or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressures to the appropriate specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (h) Check the Data List. NOTICE: - It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. - When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. - Record the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data transmitter ID that corresponds to each tire. (i) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. Identify the transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. Result B --  CHECK TRANSMITTER ID C --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - RECEIVER) (a) Disconnect the L89 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the S67 receiver connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TRANSMITTER ID (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor (f) Check the ID number on the identified transmitter by removing it from the tire and wheel. (g) Confirm that the ID number on the transmitter and recorded transmitter ID match. Result B --  PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Perform registration    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor/ Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. - When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ANTENNA AND RECEIVER (a) Replace the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Monitor Receiver / Transponder/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. Result B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 26 is detected, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then comes on. If the tire pressure warning light does not turn off after a little while, IDs may be incorrectly registered. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. CHECK TRANSMITTER ID HINT Perform this procedure to compare the registered IDs in the tire pressure warning ECU with the actual tire pressure warning valve and transmitter IDs, as the registered IDs may be incorrect. (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor (f) Check the ID number on the identified transmitter by removing it from the tire and wheel. (g) Confirm that the ID number on the transmitter and recorded transmitter ID match. Result B --  PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Perform registration    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ANTENNA AND RECEIVER (a) Replace the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Monitor Receiver / Transponder/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 41-C2145/45: Transmitter ID1 Error When the internal circuit of a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is malfunctioning, one of these DTCs is output. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified values    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (f) Display the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the Techstream. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each wheel at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data displayed on the Techstream (ID1 to ID5) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data was the cause of the output DTC. HINT - Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. - Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) corresponds to which tire. (h) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display's tire pressure display screen. Result NOTICE: - It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the update tire pressure data. - When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. - Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (i) When an "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data value (one of ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (j) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT - Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. - The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 65-C2169/69: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction and outputs DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK TIRES (a) Check that the tires are not flat, and have no indication of tire pressure drop. OK: Tires are normal. HINT If a tire is damaged, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter may also have been damaged at the same time. NG --  REPLACE TIRE AND TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and
IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC   See: C2141 NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT - Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. - The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The IDs of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter are registered in the tire pressure warning ECU. When the ECU detects that a transmitter ID code is not registered in the ECU, a DTC is output. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED ID CODES) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The registered transmitter ID codes are displayed on the Techstream screen. NG --  PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 76 - Receiver Error This DTC is stored when referencing data becomes impossible due to an internal malfunction in the ECU. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Clear the DTC    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Check for DTC. Result B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 77 - Initialization not Completed Initialization is necessary if one of the following occurs: 1) the ECU is replaced; 2) the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is replaced; 3) tires with a different standard tire pressure are installed; 4) the tires are rotated; or 5) a new vehicle is delivered. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check for the conditions described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may turn on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located around or in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings, or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  GO TO C2121/21 to C2125/25 FLOWCHART  See: C2121

dtcs.description: 79 - Tire Pressure Monitor ECU Communication Stop The tire pressure warning ECU exchanges speed signals and warning light signals with the combination meter via CAN communication. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Select "CAN Bus Check" from the System Selection Menu screen and follow the prompts on the screen to inspect the CAN Bus. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System. Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System A -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) After turning the engine switch on (IG), wait for 10 seconds or more and then check if DTC C2179/79 is output. Result B --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives speed signals from the combination meter via CAN communication. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is output only in test mode. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE s/CAN Communication System OK -- Continue to next step. s/Electronically Controlled Brake System OK -- Continue to next step. s/Meter / Gauge System OK -- Continue to next step.

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Select "CAN Bus Check" from the System Selection Menu screen, and follow the prompts on the screen, to inspect the CAN Bus. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLE SHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System. Result NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK DTC (BRAKE CONTROL SYSTEM) (a) Check if DTCs for the brake control system are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: No DTCs for the brake control system are output. NG --  GO TO BRAKE CONTROL SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK DTC (METER / GAUGE SYSTEM) (a) Check if DTCs for the meter / gauge system are output    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: No DTCs for the meter / gauge system are output. NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK VEHICLE SPEED SIGNAL (DATA LIST) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List / (e) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The vehicle speed displayed on the Techstream changes depending on the actual vehicle speed. NG --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  GO TO TEST MODE PROCEDURE  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure
